instruction: Please check the next 7 days in "San Francisco" and list only the dates that are expected to have rain. Save one date per line to "$HOME/Desktop/weather_rain_dates_3_1.txt" in YYYY-MM-DD format. If none of the next 7 days has rain, write exactly: none
reference_command:
geo=$(curl -s 'https://geocoding-api.open-meteo.com/v1/search?name=San+Francisco&count=1&language=en&format=json')
lat=$(echo "$geo" | jq -r '.results[0].latitude')
lon=$(echo "$geo" | jq -r '.results[0].longitude')
forecast=$(curl -s "https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast?latitude=${lat}&longitude=${lon}&daily=precipitation_sum&timezone=auto&forecast_days=7")
lines=$(echo "$forecast" | jq -r '.daily.time as $t | .daily.precipitation_sum as $p | [range(0; ($t|length)) | select(($p[.] // 0) > 0) | $t[.]] | .[]')
if [ -z "$lines" ]; then
  echo 'none' > "$HOME/Desktop/weather_rain_dates_3_1.txt"
else
  printf '%s\n' "$lines" > "$HOME/Desktop/weather_rain_dates_3_1.txt"
fi

parameters.expected_command:
geo=$(curl -s 'https://geocoding-api.open-meteo.com/v1/search?name=San+Francisco&count=1&language=en&format=json')
lat=$(echo "$geo" | jq -r '.results[0].latitude')
lon=$(echo "$geo" | jq -r '.results[0].longitude')
forecast=$(curl -s "https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast?latitude=${lat}&longitude=${lon}&daily=precipitation_sum&timezone=auto&forecast_days=7")
lines=$(echo "$forecast" | jq -r '.daily.time as $t | .daily.precipitation_sum as $p | [range(0; ($t|length)) | select(($p[.] // 0) > 0) | $t[.]] | .[]')
if [ -z "$lines" ]; then
  echo 'none'
else
  printf '%s\n' "$lines"
fi
